newsArray.title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi’s India debut: Why the 15-year-old’s selection has divided Indian cricket
newsArray.description: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's India debut remains the biggest talking point ahead of the T20I series against England after the 15-year-old batting prodigy was left out during the Ireland tour. While assistant coach Ryan ten Doeschate insists Sooryavanshi is "ready" but must follow the selection process, Sunil Gavaskar and Graeme Swann have backed an immediate debut. Here's a look at what India's team management, former cricketers and the teenage sensation himself have said about the growing selection debate.
creator: saurabh ganguly

newsArray.title: Hardik Pandya Rents Property in Bengaluru, Makes BCCI CoE His Permanent Training Base
newsArray.description: Star Indian all-rounder Hardik Pandya, 32, has made a landmark decision, permanently relocating to Bengaluru to establish the BCCI's Centre of Excellence (COE) as his long-term training base. This unprecedented move aims to provide him with consistent access to world-class facilities for injury recovery and skill enhancement.
creator: sports desk

newsArray.title: India replace Yastika with Kamalini, retain Shreyanka for Asian Games title defence
newsArray.description: India have made just one change to their squad for the women’s cricket competition at the 2026 Asian Games, replacing out-of-form wicketkeeper-batter Yastika Bhatia with 17-year-old G Kamalini after their group-stage exit from the ICC Women's T20 World Cup.

newsArray.title: Will Vaibhav Sooryavanshi Make His India Debut in IND vs ENG 1st T20I? Shreyas Iyer Breaks Silence on Selection Call | WATCH VIDEO
newsArray.description: Although Team India suffered a shocking series defeat to Ireland, three months after their second T20 World Cup victory, there is hardly any time for them to lick their wounds, given England are waiting in the wings. newsArray.title: Indian Women’s Cricket Team Announced for Asian Games, Harmanpreet to Lead
newsArray.description: New Delhi: The Indian women’s cricket team for the Asian Games has been announced, with Harmanpreet Kaur retaining the captaincy and Smriti Mandhana named vice-captain. Two players who were part of the recent World Cup campaign have been left out of the squad. The selectors have opted for a balanced mix of experience and young [...] The post Indian Women’s Cricket Team Announced for Asian Games, Harmanpreet to Lead first appeared on LIBERAL TV .
